title: podman load fails in a cross environment using docker
body:
/kind feature

I want to run this command inside a docker container
```
podman --root $TMP/appfs/containers load --input $SRCOCI
```
The goal is to generate an unpacked container which I want to copy to an embedded controller.  
Thus I'm in a cross building environment and do not really want to be root.

I get this error
```
+ podman --root /tmp/tmp.ddqTLtnoM3/appfs/containers load --input build/tmp-glibc/deploy/images/pcm31/AMC300-PCM31_CODESYS3.5.15.20_v1.0.0.0local75-g3525e1a.oci
cannot clone: Operation not permitted
Error: cannot re-exec process
```
Trying with sudo
```
+ sudo podman --root /tmp/tmp.lfjsAIT1Kr/appfs/containers load --input build/tmp-glibc/deploy/images/pcm31/AMC300-PCM31_CODESYS3.5.15.20_v1.0.0.0local75-g3525e1a.oci
Error: 'overlay' is not supported over overlayfs, a mount_program is required: backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver

```



**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
It seems that the problem also exist just running `podman version`

Create this Dockerfile
```
FROM debian:10.4

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get -q update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    	ca-certificates \
	gnupg \
        wget 

RUN echo 'deb http://deb.debian.org/debian buster-backports main' >> /etc/apt/sources.list \
    && echo 'deb https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/Debian_10/ /' > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.list \
    && wget -O- https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/Debian_10/Release.key | apt-key add - \
    && apt-get -q update \
    && apt-get -y -t buster-backports install libseccomp2 podman

RUN podman version
```

```
docker build .
```

**Describe the results you received:**
 ```
---> Running in c6094608be98
Error: 'overlay' is not supported over overlayfs, a mount_program is required: backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver
The command '/bin/sh -c podman version' returned a non-zero code: 125
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
$ podman version
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I assume that podman does some check on startup, that it is able to run the container.  
But I do not need that. 

I have success doing the load and copying it to my controller. The problem arrives when I try to add the commands to CI.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Error: 'overlay' is not supported over overlayfs, a mount_program is required: backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver
The command '/bin/sh -c podman info --debug' returned a non-zero code: 125
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
Error: 'overlay' is not supported over overlayfs, a mount_program is required: backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver
The command '/bin/sh -c podman info --debug' returned a non-zero code: 125
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
Listing...
podman/unknown,now 100:3.0.1-2 amd64 [installed]
podman/unknown 100:3.0.1-2 arm64
podman/unknown 100:3.0.1-2 armhf
podman/unknown 100:3.0.1-2 ppc64el
podman/unknown 100:3.0.1-2 s390x
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

I dont know how close the Docker recipe is to the latest.

The trouble shooting guide and all articlesfound via Google assumes that I want to do a `podman run`

comments.body: @giuseppe Any idea what is going on here?
We need to use fuse-overlayfs inside of the container.  Is the container configured for it?

I just did this in my homedir, is this what you are attemping?

```
podman (buildah) $ podman save --format oci-archive alpine -o /tmp/foobar.oci
podman (buildah) $ file /tmp/foobar.oci 
/tmp/foobar.oci: POSIX tar archive
podman (buildah) $ podman --root /tmp/dan/containers load --input /tmp/foobar.oci 
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob a4fd5de14e9e done  
Copying config 781257a41f done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Loaded image(s): localhost/alpine
podman (buildah) $ ls /tmp/dan/containers/
libpod  mounts  overlay  overlay-containers  overlay-images  overlay-layers  storage.lock  tmp  userns.lock
```

@giuseppe after seeing similar problems with buildah I found that running the fedora image from quay.io worked better.   
But that did not help me with podman

```
$ docker run -it --rm --security-opt seccomp=unconfined     --security-opt apparmor=unconfined      quay.io/podman/stable:latest podman version
Error: mount /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: operation not permitted
```

Do I need some modification of my docker runner?

Also, is some fixing needed inside the container? The rest of my Dockerfiles are based on debian tool chain, thus I would prefer to have a apt based Docker recipe.

I found a solution by changing the driver to vfs rather than overlay
```
sed -i -e 's|^driver = "overlay"|driver = "vfs"|g' /etc/containers/storage.conf
```

This works out of the box for docker, for both `podman` and `buildah`,   
but what are the drawbacks of this?
vfs is just a very slow and disk intensive driver.  Basically each layer is an entire copy of the underlying layer + changes.

As I only plan to put a single layer container on my controller, I can live with that.

However I may face this problem again in the future. So I'm quite curious about what the difference is in the Docker setups on Fedora and Ubuntu.
Not really sure of the question, but for what you are attempting to do, there is no difference between Fedora and Ubuntu.
@rhatdan  I'm wondering why the command in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9701#issuecomment-797532184 fails.

Try 

docker run --user=podman --device=/dev/fuse -it --rm --security-opt seccomp=unconfined     --security-opt apparmor=unconfined      quay.io/podman/stable:latest podman version

Or

docker run --cap-add SYS_ADMIN -it --rm --security-opt seccomp=unconfined     --security-opt apparmor=unconfined      quay.io/podman/stable:latest podman version

If you want to mount a file system as root, you need to have CAP_SYSADMIN, or run with a user namespace and mount namespace.

```
sh-5.1# docker run --user=podman --device=/dev/fuse -it --rm --security-opt seccomp=unconfined     --security-opt apparmor=unconfined      quay.io/podman/stable:latest podman version
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.8
Built:        Fri Feb 19 16:56:17 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
sh-5.1# docker run --cap-add cap-sysadmin -it --rm --security-opt seccomp=unconfined     --security-opt apparmor=unconfined      quay.io/podman/stable:latest podman version
docker: Error response from daemon: invalid CapAdd: unknown capability: "CAP_CAP-SYSADMIN".
See 'docker run --help'.
sh-5.1# docker run --cap-add SYS_ADMIN -it --rm --security-opt seccomp=unconfined     --security-opt apparmor=unconfined      quay.io/podman/stable:latest podman version
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.8
Built:        Fri Feb 19 16:56:17 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
